blocks.summary: Inline callout banner with variant, tone, and dismiss controls; composes `.cui-surface`.
blocks.description:
Light-DOM callout for inline status messages. Renders an icon column,
a body column, and an optional dismiss column in a three-column grid.
Use it for non-blocking feedback (success, info, warning, error) that
don't stop the user; for blocking feedback, use `cui-modal` instead.

The default appearance is a bordered card with an accent rail on the
inline-start edge. `[data-variant]` changes the accent hue; `[data-tone]`
switches betweeen the default outline look, a subtle fille background,
and a solid filled background where the accent becomes the surface.

**Contextual markup.** The alert composes `.cui-surface` for its chrome
(background, border, radius, padding — shadow suppressed) while keeping
its own icon · body · dismiss grid and the inline-start accent rail. Add
`.cui-surface` alongside `.cui-alert` in the markup. The
body is a `.cui-stack`; the icon, title, message, and dismiss button keep
their classes (the icon is bespoke, the title / message are JS hooks the
`toast()` runtime reads, the dismiss button pairs with `data-cui-dismiss`).
An actions row is a `.cui-cluster`.

blocks.name: cui-alert-icon
blocks.summary: Circular icon badge for the alert's leading column.
blocks.description:
Icon column inside `.cui-alert`. A small circular tinted badge that
consumes `--cui-alert-accent` for its background and color. Wrap a
single short glyph or letter; mark the element `aria-hidden="true"`
if the icon is decorative (the alert's accessible name should come
from the title/message text).

blocks.name: cui-alert-title
blocks.summary: Bold lead line inside the alert body.
blocks.description:
Bold lead line at the top of the alert body. Pick the semantic element to match the alert's role on the page (`

` for inline messages, a heading element when the alert is a page-level banner) — the class only handles font weight, not the document outline. The `toast()` runtime reads this class to populate the toast heading.

blocks.name: cui-alert-message
blocks.summary: Message text paragraph(s) inside the alert body.
blocks.description:
Message text inside the alert body. Applies implicitly to any `

` child of the body (no class required); use the explicit `.cui-alert-message` class on non-paragraph elements such as a `` for a one-line message or a `

` wrapping multiple lines. The `toast()` runtime reads this class to populate the toast message.

blocks.name: cui-alert-dismiss
blocks.summary: Trailing dismiss button on the alert.
blocks.description:
Dismiss button in the trailing column of `.cui-alert`. Render as
`<button type="button" aria-label="Dismiss">×</button>` — the
visible glyph is decorative, the accessible name comes from
`aria-label`. Pair it with `data-cui-dismiss` so the toast runtime's
global click handler removes the alert; this class is style-only.

Label / legend for a `.cui-article-filter` row. Muted, bold,

blocks.name: cui-article-filter-chip
blocks.summary: Filter pill with visually-hidden input; `:has(input:checked)` flips to accent.
blocks.description:
Individual filter chip. Wraps a visually-hidden `<input>`
(checkbox or radio) plus the visible label content. Uses
`:has(input:checked)` to flip the chip to the accent fill
when toggled — modern CSS-only state, no JS required for the
visual side. The `cui-article-filter` web component handles
the show/hide logic on the targeted cards.

region so it stays focusable and announced by screen readers;
focus on the input draws an outline on the chip via

blocks.description:
Light-DOM button block. Apply directly to a `<button>` or `<a>`
element when the markup is already semantic; pair with the
matching `cui-button` web component when you need disabled-state
forwarding or anchor/button polymorphism with JS-controlled
attributes. The class declares its own foreground / background /

blocks.description:
Contact card — a single point of contact: a department, an office desk, a person's direct line, or a general "get in touch" panel. Stacks an optional eyebrow, a title, a short lead, a list of contact methods, and an actions row. Shares its surface tokens with `.cui-content-card` so it sits cleanly beside feature, case, and person cards in mixed layouts; drop several into a `.cui-grid` for a contacts directory. **Contextual markup.** Compose `.cui-surface` for the card chrome and `.cui-stack` for the body rhythm; the card styles its own descendants, so each part carries only the canonical primitive (or a semantic element): - `.cui-eyebrow` — the team / department label ("Support", "Press"). - `.cui-display` — the title, retuned to card scale in place. - a bare `

` — the muted lead / intro paragraph. - a `.cui-contact-methods` `

` — the label / value method list (shared with `.cui-location-card`; see its own block). - `.cui-cluster` — the actions row.

blocks.summary: Location card pairing a map / photo with address and details; composes `.cui-surface` + a `.cui-stack` body.
blocks.description:
Location card — an office, branch, or venue: a media panel (map,
photo, or embedded `cui-map` / `<iframe>`) paired with a body
holding the name, address, contact methods, and directions. Lays
the media beside the body at wider widths and stacks it on top at
narrow widths; `[data-orient="stacked"]` pins the media on top at
every width. Reuses `.cui-contact-methods` for hours / phone, so
a location card and a contact card read consistently.

**Contextual markup.** Compose `.cui-surface` for the chrome and a
`.cui-stack` body; the card zeroes its own padding (the media bleeds)
and the body owns the inset. The body styles its own descendants:

- `.cui-location-card-media` — the map / photo / embed panel (a
  bespoke fixed-column / banner region; kept).
- `.cui-display` — the location name, retuned to card scale.
- an `<address>` — the postal address (reset to plain, muted text).
- a `.cui-contact-methods` `<dl>` — hours / phone (the shared list).
- `.cui-cluster` — the actions row.

Feature card — icon + heading + paragraph, designed to call out
a single feature, value prop, or step. Compose `.cui-surface` in
markup for the shared bordered + elevated chrome (the card maps the
`--cui-content-card-*` knobs onto the surface override vars), as do
`.cui-content-card`, `.cui-stat`, `.cui-logo-item`, and
`.cui-case-card`, so they all visually match in a mixed grid.

`.cui-content-card` is the generic sibling — same chrome and
padding, no icon slot — use it when the card is a free-form
content block. `.cui-stat` is the KPI variant. `.cui-logo-item`
is the brand-logo cell. `.cui-case-card` adds a media region
(see its own entry).

blocks.summary: Native `<dialog>` styling with size + side-sheet variants; composes `.cui-surface`.
blocks.description:
Modal dialog block, built directly on the native `<dialog>`
element. Backdrop dismiss, ESC handling, and focus management
come from the platform; this class adds visual styling, a
responsive content layout, four sizes, and a side-sheet variant.
Pair with the `cui-modal` web component when you need remote-
trigger control (`data-cui-modal-target`) or typed close events.

The dialog max-inline-size is driven by
`--cui-dialog-max-inline-size`, which `[data-size]` overrides.
The `sheet-end` variant pins the dialog to the inline-end edge
as a full-height drawer.

**Contextual markup.** The dialog composes `.cui-surface` for its
chrome (background, border, radius, shadow, padding) while keeping
its own sizing, centering, and `::backdrop`. Add `.cui-surface` to
the markup; the `--cui-dialog-*` knobs feed the matching
`--cui-surface-*` vars.

blocks.summary: Bordered disclosure card with custom marker; wraps `<details>`. Composes `.cui-surface`.
blocks.description:
Disclosure block — a bordered card wrapping a native `<details>`
(or a non-`<details>` container with a `.cui-disclosure-summary`
child for cases where `<details>` semantics don't fit). Apply the
class directly to `<details>` for the simplest case; pair with
the `cui-disclosure` web component when you need remote-trigger
control or attribute-driven open state. The custom CSS marker
replaces the native triangle and rotates on `[open]`.

**Contextual markup.** A default disclosure composes `.cui-surface`
for its card chrome (background, border, radius — shadow suppressed,
padding zeroed since the summary/body pad themselves). The `ghost`
variant needs no surface; it strips to a single bottom border.

Stack multiple disclosures inside a `.cui-stack` for FAQ patterns.
Combine `data-variant="ghost"` on each disclosure with
`data-variant="stacked"` on the `.cui-stack` for the borderless FAQ
list (replaces the former `.cui-disclosure-group`).

blocks.name: cui-disclosure-eyebrow
blocks.summary: Muted uppercase kicker for disclosure metadata.
blocks.description:
Small-caps kicker text above a disclosure summary or inside the
body. Renders as muted, uppercase, slightly-tracked metadata.
Use for a category label ("FAQ", "Shipping", "Account") above
the question text.

blocks.name: cui-disclosure-body
blocks.summary: Padded body area below the disclosure summary.
blocks.description:
Content area below the disclosure summary. Provides the bottom
and horizontal padding to match the summary's inline padding,
leaving the visual gap between summary and body up to the
disclosure's own layout. Wrap the answer markup (paragraphs,
lists, embedded media) here.

Reduced-motion users get the body as soon as the disclosure
opens; others see a 160ms ease-in fade-and-slide.

blocks.summary: Event card with prominent date, title, meta, status pill; composes `.cui-surface` and styles its own descendants.
blocks.description:
Event card — meeting, workshop, conference, or community session. A flex container that pairs a prominent date block with a body column holding category, title, time, location, excerpt, and actions. The date sits beside the body and collapses below it at narrow widths; use `[data-orient="stacked"]` to pin the date on top for poster-style listings. Carries a `data-status` of `upcoming`, `ongoing`, `past`, `cancelled`, or `sold-out`. The status both colors a small corner pill and tones the date block automatically — past events drop to muted, cancelled events strike through the title, sold-out events tint the status pill warning. Wire it to data from a CMS; the optional `cui-calendar` / `cui-day-planner` components read the first `` (the date block) for the start, an optional second `` for the end, and the `[data-cui-event-title]` element for the label and link. **Contextual markup.** Compose `.cui-surface` for the card chrome and a `.cui-stack` body; the card styles its own descendants, so each part carries only the canonical primitive (or nothing at all): - `.cui-event-card-date` — the bespoke date block (a `` with month / day / year spans); kept, since the calendar / day-planner read it. - `.cui-eyebrow` — the category label (wrap a child `` to link). - `.cui-display[data-cui-event-title]` — the title (wrap a child `` so it is clickable). Retuned to card scale in place; the `data-cui-event-title` hook lets the calendar / day-planner read it. - `.cui-meta` — the time / location / capacity row; the first entry (the time) reads at full strength. - a bare `

` — the excerpt. - `.cui-cluster` — the actions row. - a direct-child `` — the status pill (toned by `[data-status]`). Lay cards out with `.cui-grid` (`--cui-grid-min: 22rem`) or stack them in a `.cui-stack`.

the machine-readable datetime stays attached to the human label;
the calendar / day-planner read it as the event's start.

blocks.summary: Shared base for `.cui-input` / `.cui-select` / `.cui-textarea`.
blocks.description:
Shared base styling for native form controls — applies to
`.cui-input` (on `<input>`), `.cui-select` (on `<select>`), and
`.cui-textarea` (on `<textarea>`). Same border, padding, focus
ring, and disabled/read-only/invalid states for all three; the
`:where()` keeps specificity at zero so individual rules can
override without escalation.

Each class also gets element-specific tweaks: `.cui-textarea`
adds `min-block-size: 6rem` and `resize: vertical`; `.cui-select`
paints a custom chevron and removes the native arrow via
`appearance: none`. Invalid state is driven by `:user-invalid`
(native validation) plus `cui-field[data-invalid] :where(…)`
(when wrapped in a `cui-field` web component with server-side
errors).

blocks.name: cui-check
blocks.summary: Wrapping label for native checkbox / radio inputs.
blocks.description:
Wrapping `<label>` for native checkbox or radio inputs — places
the input and its visible label side-by-side with `--cui-space-2`
between them. Uses `accent-color` to brand the native checkbox
with `--cui-color-accent`. No bespoke styling on the input
itself, so the experience matches platform conventions
(including indeterminate, focus rings, and OS-level scaling).

blocks.summary: Toggle switch wrapper around a hidden checkbox.
blocks.description:
Toggle switch — a styled wrapper around a visually-hidden
checkbox plus a `.cui-switch-track`. The input itself is
absolute-positioned and clipped (still focusable and announced
by screen readers); the visual switch is the `.cui-switch-track`
sibling whose appearance changes via `:checked` and
`:focus-visible` on the input.

blocks.name: cui-switch-track
blocks.summary: Pill-shaped track with sliding knob; child of `.cui-switch`.
blocks.description:
Visual track and knob of a `.cui-switch`. A 2.5rem × 1.5rem pill
with a 1.25rem circular knob (a `::after` pseudo-element) that
translates inline-end-ward when the input sibling is `:checked`.
Always paired with the parent `.cui-switch` label and an
immediately-preceding `<input type="checkbox">` — standalone use
is not supported.

blocks.name: cui-field-label

Standalone field label — bold, full-width, with `--cui-space-1`
bottom margin to gap from the control. Use on `<label>` elements
paired with `.cui-input` / `.cui-select` / `.cui-textarea`.
Inside the `cui-field` web component, the `label` slot already
has this styling — use this class only outside the component.

blocks.name: cui-field-help
blocks.summary: Muted helper text below a form control.
blocks.description:
Helper text below a form control — muted, slightly larger than
`.cui-field-error`, with a small top margin. Use for non-error
guidance ("We never share your email", "Use at least 12
characters"). For validation errors, use `.cui-field-error`
instead.

blocks.name: cui-field-error
blocks.summary: Inline error message in `--cui-color-danger`.
blocks.description:
Error message below a form control — same size as
`.cui-field-help` but tinted with `--cui-color-danger`. Use for
inline validation errors when not using the `cui-field` web
component. Inside `cui-field`, the `error` and `error-<key>`
slots are already styled — this class is for the standalone
form pattern.

blocks.summary: Hero band; composes canonical primitives for the content + optional media; variant modifiers.
blocks.description:
Hero band — the top-of-page opener with a content column and optional media, background image, or overlay. Pure CSS; apply `.cui-hero` to a `
`. **Contextual markup.** The band styles its own descendants, so the content carries only canonical primitives — no bespoke slot classes: - `.cui-hero-inner` — the width-capped grid (content + optional media). - a `.cui-stack` — the content column (eyebrow, title, copy, actions), capped to the content width and retuned in place. - `.cui-eyebrow` — the kicker above the title. - `.cui-display` — the headline, at hero (display) scale. - a bare `

` — lead copy, rendered larger and muted. - `.cui-cluster` — the actions row. - `.cui-hero-media` — the optional image / video wrapper. For the background variant, add a `.cui-hero-bg` element and set its image with an inline custom property: `style="--cui-hero-background-image: url('/hero.jpg')"`. Modifiers compose on the container via data-attributes: the `data-variant` layout (`split` — content + media side by side; `text` — no media; `overlay` — media behind the copy; `background` — background image), `data-media="start"` (media leads), `data-align="center"`, and the content width `data-width="narrow | default | wide"`.

Background layer for the `background` variant. An absolutely-positioned

blocks.description:
Width-capped inner wrapper inside `.cui-hero`. Centers the content column
and optional media to the active container width (set per `data-width`) and
supplies the column gap. Load-bearing: the hero is a full-bleed band, so the
centered, width-capped grid must live on an inner element.

blocks.summary: Card with media region and content body; composes `.cui-surface` and styles its own descendants.
blocks.description:
Media card — a bordered, elevated card with media on top and a body column below. Shares the `--cui-content-card-*` token family with the rest of the card system, so it visually matches feature / case / person / article cards in mixed layouts. Set `[data-orient="row"]` to pair media beside body, similar to the article-card row variant. **Contextual markup.** Compose `.cui-surface` for the card chrome and a `.cui-stack` body; the card styles its own descendants, so each part carries only the canonical primitive (or nothing at all): - `.cui-media-card-media` (or a bare first-child media element) — the media region. - a `.cui-stack` — the body (eyebrow, heading, copy, meta, actions). - `.cui-eyebrow` — the kicker above the title. - a bare heading — the title (default heading scale). - a bare `

` — the description, rendered muted. - `.cui-metadata` — the date / reading-time / byline line. - `.cui-cluster` — the actions row. Lay cards out with `.cui-grid`.

blocks.name: cui-media-card-media
blocks.summary: Media region inside a media card (covers bare first/last-child media too).
blocks.description:
Media region of `.cui-media-card`. The combined selector with
`.cui-media-card > :where(img, video, picture, iframe, svg):is(:first-child, :last-child)`
means a bare media element gets the same styling whether it is the first
or the last child — so swapping the source order of the body and media
reverses the layout (image-first vs text-first) with no extra markup.

blocks.summary: Media block with text overlay and scrim.
blocks.description:
Media overlay block — a background image (or video) with text
overlaid on top, separated by a configurable scrim. Use for
hero cards, feature blocks where the image and copy share the
same surface. Block-axis alignment of the body defaults to the
end (text reads from the bottom up); `[data-align]` controls
both the block-axis and inline-axis alignment.

The scrim is a `::before` gradient by default (transparent at
top, opaque at bottom). `[data-scrim]` swaps it for a solid
tint, a top-anchored gradient, or removes it entirely.

blocks.name: cui-media-overlay-media
blocks.summary: Absolutely-positioned background media inside an overlay.
blocks.description:
Background media element inside `.cui-media-overlay`. The
combined selector with
`.cui-media-overlay > :where(img, video, picture, iframe, svg):first-child`
means a bare first-child media element gets the same absolute-
positioned full-cover styling — class is optional. The media
sits below both the scrim (`::before`) and the body content via
a z-index of -2.

blocks.name: cui-nav
blocks.summary: Responsive nav container — vertical on mobile, horizontal at ≥48rem.
blocks.description:
Vertical (mobile) → horizontal (≥48rem) navigation container.
Holds `.cui-nav-link` items and optional `.cui-dropdown`s; the
`cui-navbar` web component uses it as the default `slot="nav"`
layout, but it also works standalone as a side rail or footer
nav.

blocks.name: cui-nav-link
blocks.summary: Nav link with hover and `[aria-current="page"]` emphasis states.
blocks.description:
Nav link primitive. Default state is muted; `:hover` and
`[aria-current="page"]` both apply the same emphasis treatment
(full-strength color over a muted surface), and `aria-current`
additionally bolds the text. Set `aria-current="page"` on the
link matching the active page — the same attribute the docs
site sets via the `current` partial query parameter.

blocks.summary: Dropdown wrapper for a toggle + menu pair; `[data-open]` reveals.
blocks.description:
Dropdown container — a `.cui-dropdown-toggle` followed by a
`.cui-dropdown-menu` sibling, both inside this wrapper. Set
`[data-open]` on the dropdown (the `cui-navbar` web component
or your own JS) to reveal the menu, rotate the chevron, and
trigger the entry animation. Menus open below by default; on
viewports ≥48rem they absolute-position relative to the
wrapper, and nested menus flip to flyout to the inline-end side.

Reduced-motion users get an instant open with no transform.

blocks.name: cui-dropdown-toggle
blocks.summary: Toggle button with rotating chevron inside `.cui-dropdown`.
blocks.description:
Toggle button inside `.cui-dropdown`. A bare button with a
CSS-drawn chevron that rotates 180° when the parent dropdown
has `[data-open]`. The button itself doesn't manage open state
— the `cui-navbar` web component (or your JS) toggles
`[data-open]` on the parent `.cui-dropdown` and `aria-expanded`
on this button in response to clicks.

blocks.name: cui-dropdown-menu
blocks.summary: Flyout list inside `.cui-dropdown`; reveal via parent `[data-open]`. Composes `.cui-surface`.
blocks.description:
Flyout menu inside `.cui-dropdown`. Hidden by default (zero
opacity, translated down, `display: none` via `[hidden]` for
accessibility); the parent's `[data-open]` reveals it with a
shared fade-and-slide. Use `<ul>` semantics with
`.cui-dropdown-item` children and optional `.cui-dropdown-divider`
separators. Nested menus open to the inline-end on viewports
≥48rem.

Composes `.cui-surface` for its chrome (border, radius, background,
shadow, padding) — add `.cui-surface` alongside `.cui-dropdown-menu`
in markup — while keeping its own grid layout and reveal animation.

blocks.name: cui-dropdown-item
blocks.summary: Menu item with hover and `[aria-current="page"]` states.
blocks.description:
Item inside `.cui-dropdown-menu`. Sized to be touch-comfortable
(2.25rem min-height) with the same hover treatment as
`.cui-nav-link`. Use `<a>` for navigation items; use `<button>`
if you want an action that doesn't change the URL.

blocks.name: cui-dropdown-divider
blocks.summary: Horizontal separator between dropdown item groups.
blocks.description:
Visual separator between groups of items in a dropdown menu.
Render as `<hr class="cui-dropdown-divider">` between
`.cui-dropdown-item`s.

blocks.summary: Responsive site-nav band: centered bar that collapses behind a burger below 48rem.
blocks.description:
Full-width navbar band wrapping a centered `.cui-navbar-inner`. Below 48rem
the nav + actions collapse behind a burger toggle; at ≥48rem the bar and
collapse sit side by side on one row and the burger is hidden.

The `cui-navbar` custom element enhances this markup — it injects the
`.cui-navbar-toggle` burger button and wires up collapse/dropdown/sticky
behavior + ARIA — but every visual is driven by these classes, so the navbar
lays out identically before and after the element upgrades (no FOUC). The
layout also works standalone as a plain `.cui-navbar` (without the burger or
JS) for static, always-expanded use.

Structure:
  .cui-navbar > .cui-navbar-inner
    > .cui-navbar-bar         (brand + injected burger)
    > .cui-navbar-collapse
      > .cui-navbar-collapse-panel  (.cui-navbar-nav + .cui-navbar-actions)

blocks.name: cui-navbar-inner
blocks.summary: Width-capped, centered wrapper for the navbar bar + collapse.
blocks.description:
Width-capped inner wrapper inside `.cui-navbar`. Centers the bar and the
collapse panel to the container width and supplies the block padding.

Always-visible top row of the navbar: brand on one side, the burger
toggle (and any inline actions) on the other.

blocks.name: cui-navbar-brand
blocks.summary: Inline-flex brand link in the navbar bar.
blocks.description:
Brand link/lockup in the navbar bar. An inline-flex anchor that keeps the
logo + wordmark on one line and inherits the bar's color.

blocks.name: cui-navbar-toggle
blocks.summary: Burger button revealing the collapsed nav below 48rem.
blocks.description:
Burger toggle injected into `.cui-navbar-bar` by the `cui-navbar` element.
Hidden at ≥48rem where the collapse panel is always open.

blocks.name: cui-navbar-toggle-lines
blocks.summary: Three-bar burger glyph for the navbar toggle.
blocks.description:
The three burger bars inside `.cui-navbar-toggle`, drawn with the element
plus its `::before` / `::after` pseudo-elements.

blocks.name: cui-navbar-collapse
blocks.summary: Animated collapse region revealed when the navbar is expanded.
blocks.description:
Animated collapse region below the bar. Collapsed to zero height by
default; the `cui-navbar` element opens it via `[expanded]`, animating
grid-template-rows, opacity, and transform. The `[animation="fade"]` and
`[animation="none"]` attributes on the host tune or disable the motion.

blocks.name: cui-navbar-collapse-panel
blocks.summary: Padded inner stack inside the collapse region.
blocks.description:
Inner padding wrapper for the collapse region. Stacks the nav list and
actions and clips overflow while the panel animates open.

blocks.name: cui-navbar-nav
blocks.summary: Navigation-link and action stacks inside the collapse panel.
blocks.description:
The two stacks inside the collapse panel: `.cui-navbar-nav` for the
primary links and `.cui-navbar-actions` for buttons/CTAs. Both stack
vertically below 48rem and lay out inline at wider widths.

blocks.summary: Page-intro band; composes canonical primitives for the content + optional aside; variant modifiers.
blocks.description:
Page-intro band — the lighter alternative to a hero for case-study, vacancy, and article pages. Pure CSS; apply `.cui-page-intro` to a `
`. It is the full-bleed, width-capped surface; inside it a `.cui-page-intro-layout` centers a content column and an optional aside. **Contextual markup.** The band styles its own descendants, so the content carries only canonical primitives — no bespoke slot classes: - `.cui-page-intro-layout` — the width-capped grid (content + aside). - a `.cui-stack` — the content column (eyebrow, title, meta, copy, actions). Capped to a reading width and retuned in place. - `.cui-eyebrow` — the kicker above the title. - `.cui-display` — the headline, retuned to page-intro scale in place. - `.cui-meta` — the optional metadata row (sector, year, reading time). - a bare `

` — lead copy, rendered muted at reading measure. - `.cui-cluster` — the actions row. - `.cui-surface.cui-aside` — the optional summary rail. Modifiers compose on the container via data-attributes: the `data-variant` type (`landing`, `case`, `vacancy`, `campaign`, `full-bleed`), the `data-tone="inverse"` treatment, and `data-align="center"`.

Width-capped layout grid inside `.cui-page-intro`. Centers the content and
optional aside; the surface reaches the band edges while the copy stays
inset by the gutter. Becomes two columns for the case/vacancy/campaign
variants at ≥56rem.

blocks.summary: Navigation block for server-rendered, crawlable page links.
blocks.description:
Pagination — a navigation block for paging through a long list
(search results, a blog index, a `/vacatures` overview). Built
for **server-rendered, crawlable** paging: each page is a real
`<a href>` so the control works without JS and search engines can
follow it. Wrap the links in a `<nav aria-label="Paginering">`
with an inner `<ul>`; mark the current
page with `aria-current="page"` and disabled prev / next with
`aria-disabled="true"` (on a `<span>`) — there is no clickable
target for an unavailable direction. The `<ul>` and its `<li>`
rows are styled contextually — no wrapper class is needed.

Centered by default; `[data-align]` pins it start / end, and
`[data-variant="compact"]` hides the numbered pages for a bare
prev / next + status pager on small screens.

blocks.name: cui-pagination-link

variants.description: Maps to `[data-control]`. Widens the target and pairs the label with a directional arrow.

variants.name: next
variants.description: Maps to `[data-control]`. Widens the target and pairs the label with a directional arrow.

blocks.summary: Page number or prev / next control; accent fill when current, muted when disabled.
blocks.description:
Page link / control inside a pagination `<li>`. Works as an
`<a href>` for a navigable page or a `<span>` for the
unavailable direction. Square-ish min target (`--cui-pagination-size`)
for numbered pages; prev / next (`[data-control]`) grow to fit
their label. `aria-current="page"` paints the active page in the
accent fill; `aria-disabled="true"` / `:disabled` mutes and
disables the control.

blocks.name: cui-pagination-ellipsis
blocks.summary: Decorative "…" gap for skipped page ranges.
blocks.description:
Ellipsis gap inside a pagination `<li>` — the "…" standing in
for skipped page ranges. Decorative; put `aria-hidden="true"` on
the wrapping item so screen readers skip it.

blocks.name: cui-pagination-status
blocks.summary: Muted "Page X of Y" status text between prev / next.
blocks.description:
Page-status text inside a pagination `<li>` — "Pagina 2 van
8". Muted, padded to align with the controls; the readable
alternative to numbered pages in the `compact` variant.

blocks.description:
Editorial quote card. Compose `.cui-surface` for the card chrome:
`<figure class="cui-surface cui-quote">`. Wraps a `<blockquote>`
(or `.cui-quote-body`) with optional `.cui-quote-mark`,

Feeds the `--cui-surface-*` vars from its `--cui-quote-*` knobs

blocks.summary: Customer-testimonial card; composes `.cui-surface`.
blocks.description:
Customer-testimonial card. Compose `.cui-surface` for the card

.cui-testimonial` base — same surface tokens, same variants

are equivalent, but the name lines up with the picture in a
way readers expect from testimonial layouts. Stack the name +

blocks.summary: Segmented pill control: a rounded track of connected link/button segments.
blocks.description:
Segmented pill control — a single rounded track split into a set of
connected segments. Use it as a compact link set (a `<nav>` of `<a>`s,
one carrying `[aria-current="page"]`) or as a single-choice control (a
group of `<button>`s, the active one carrying `[aria-selected="true"]`).
The active segment lifts out of the track as a raised pill; the rest stay
muted and flush.

Segments share the track width equally by default, so the control reads as
a balanced set of pills. Horizontal by default; `[data-orient="stacked"]`
turns it into a vertical pill column (each segment full-width) for side
rails and narrow layouts.

This is a pure-CSS block — the active state is driven by attributes you set
on the segments (`aria-current` for links, `aria-selected` for buttons), so
it works with a server-rendered current page or with your own JS toggling
the attribute. For tabbed panels with keyboard/ARIA wiring, reach for
`cui-tabs` / `.cui-tablist` instead.

blocks.name: cui-segment
blocks.summary: Segment inside `.cui-segmented`; active via `[aria-current]` / `[aria-selected]`.
blocks.description:
A single segment inside `.cui-segmented`. Works as an `<a>` (link set) or a
`<button>` (single-choice control). Default state is muted and flush with
the track; the active segment — `[aria-current="page"]` for links or
`[aria-selected="true"]` for buttons — lifts to a raised pill with
full-strength text. Segments share the track width equally.

blocks.summary: Vertical side-rail nav: icon-only mini-rail on desktop, off-canvas drawer below 48rem.
blocks.description:
Vertical side-rail navigation. A full-height column of `.cui-nav-link`s
that docks to the inline-start (or inline-end via `[side="end"]`) of a
dashboard-style layout. It collapses to an icon-only mini-rail on wide
viewports and hides off-canvas behind a backdrop below 48rem.

The `cui-sidenav` custom element enhances this markup — it injects the
collapse toggle, the mobile dismiss button, and the backdrop, and wires up
the ARIA / focus-trap / scroll-lock behavior — but every visual is driven by
these classes, so the rail lays out identically before and after the element
upgrades (no FOUC). The layout also works standalone as a plain `.cui-sidenav`
(without the injected chrome or JS) for a static, always-expanded rail.

Structure:
  .cui-sidenav
    > .cui-sidenav-inner            (the visible rail surface)
      > .cui-sidenav-header         (brand + injected collapse toggle / dismiss)
      > .cui-sidenav-nav            (a column of .cui-nav-link items)
      > .cui-sidenav-footer         (actions: theme toggle, CTA…)
    > .cui-sidenav-backdrop         (injected; mobile scrim)

blocks.name: cui-sidenav-inner
blocks.summary: Padded rail surface that stacks the header, nav, and footer.
blocks.description:
The visible rail surface inside `.cui-sidenav`. Carries the background,
divider border, and padding, and stacks the header / nav / footer. Kept
separate from the host so the off-canvas backdrop (a sibling painted behind
it) doesn't tint the rail itself.

blocks.name: cui-sidenav-backdrop
blocks.summary: Mobile scrim behind the off-canvas drawer; click to dismiss.
blocks.description:
Backdrop scrim injected behind the off-canvas drawer on mobile. A fixed
full-viewport overlay that fades in while `[expanded]`; clicking it closes
the drawer (handled by the `cui-sidenav` element). Hidden at ≥48rem where
the rail is a persistent part of the layout.

blocks.name: cui-sidenav-header
blocks.summary: Rail header row holding the brand and the injected control.
blocks.description:
Top row of the rail: the brand lockup plus, when the `cui-sidenav` element
upgrades, the injected collapse toggle (desktop) or dismiss button (mobile).

blocks.name: cui-sidenav-brand
blocks.summary: Inline-flex brand lockup (logo + wordmark) at the top of the rail.
blocks.description:
Brand link/lockup at the top of the rail. An inline-flex anchor pairing a
`.cui-sidenav-logo` mark with a `.cui-sidenav-label` wordmark; the wordmark
(like every `.cui-sidenav-label`) hides in the collapsed mini-rail.

blocks.name: cui-sidenav-logo
blocks.summary: Fixed-size brand-mark slot in the rail header.
blocks.description:
Brand mark inside `.cui-sidenav-brand` — a fixed-size square slot for a logo
glyph, SVG, or image. Stays visible in the collapsed mini-rail.

blocks.name: cui-sidenav-label
blocks.summary: Collapsible text label; hidden while the rail is `[collapsed]`.
blocks.description:
Text label shared by the brand wordmark and every nav link. Hidden when the
rail is collapsed to the icon-only mini-rail, so a single class drives the
whole expand/collapse text reveal.

blocks.name: cui-sidenav-nav
blocks.summary: Scrollable vertical column of nav links inside the rail.
blocks.description:
The primary navigation list inside the rail — a vertical column of
`.cui-nav-link`s (compose the existing nav-link primitive for the link
chrome, hover, and `[aria-current="page"]` states). Grows to fill the rail
and scrolls independently when the list is long, keeping the header and
footer pinned.

blocks.name: cui-sidenav-icon
blocks.summary: Fixed-size leading icon slot in a sidenav nav link.
blocks.description:
Fixed-size icon slot at the start of a sidenav `.cui-nav-link`. Keeps icons
aligned in a column and centered when the rail collapses to the mini-rail.

blocks.name: cui-sidenav-footer
blocks.summary: Action stack pinned to the bottom of the rail.
blocks.description:
Bottom region of the rail for actions — theme toggle, CTA button, account
menu. Stacks its children and collapses out of flow when empty.

blocks.name: cui-sidenav-toggle
blocks.summary: Square button that collapses the rail to the icon-only mini-rail.
blocks.description:
Collapse toggle injected into the header by the `cui-sidenav` element. A
square control with a CSS chevron that points toward the inline-start to
collapse and flips when the rail is `[collapsed]`. Shown only at ≥48rem,
where the mini-rail makes sense; hidden in the mobile drawer.

blocks.name: cui-sidenav-dismiss
blocks.summary: Square close button for the off-canvas drawer (mobile only).
blocks.description:
Dismiss (close) button injected into the header on mobile by the `cui-sidenav`
element. A square control with a CSS-drawn cross that closes the off-canvas
drawer. Shown only below 48rem; hidden once the rail is a persistent part of
the layout.

blocks.name: cui-tablist
examples:

Pricing content.
blocks.summary: Horizontal strip of tab controls with overflow scroll.
blocks.description:
Horizontal row of `.cui-tab` controls with a bottom border that
the active tab "punches" with the accent underline. Scrolls
horizontally when tabs overflow the inline-size; `scrollbar-gutter:
stable` keeps the tablist height steady regardless of whether the
scrollbar is showing.

The `<cui-tabs>` web component lays out the tablist and panels (a
vertical stack) and wires up keyboard navigation, ARIA, and panel
visibility — its element-level grid layout lives in
`src/styles/elements.css`.

blocks.name: cui-tab
blocks.summary: Tab control; active state via `[data-selected="true"]`.
blocks.description:
Single tab control inside `.cui-tablist`. Active state is driven
by `[data-selected="true"]`, not a class — set the attribute on
the active tab to surface the accent underline, full-strength
text color, and bolder weight. The `cui-tabs` web component
manages the attribute for you; if you're not using it, mirror
the same attribute toggle in your own JS.

blocks.summary: Person card (photo, name, role, meta, bio, links); composes `.cui-surface`.
blocks.description:
Person card — a vertically stacked card with optional photo,
name, role, metadata, bio, and links. Compose `.cui-surface`
for the card chrome: `<article class="cui-surface cui-person">`,
or wrap an `<a>` to make the whole card a link (hover surfaces
an accent border). The card feeds the `--cui-surface-*` vars from
its `--cui-content-card-*` / `--cui-person-*` knobs, so it still
matches feature/case cards in mixed layouts.

Lay the body out with `.cui-stack`, the links row with
`.cui-cluster`, and a grid of cards with `.cui-grid`
(`--cui-grid-min: 16rem`; 20rem + `data-gap="spacious"` for a
leadership grid).

Photo shape is controlled by `[data-photo]`; layout by
`[data-orient]`; alignment by `[data-align]`; chrome and
proportions by `[data-variant]`. `leadership` is a one-shot
preset that boosts photo size, padding, and typography in one
step — use it on solo bios or hero people.

blocks.name: cui-person-photo
blocks.summary: Photo element inside `.cui-person` (or any first-child ``).
blocks.description: Photo element inside `.cui-person`. Apply directly to `` — a same-selector `.cui-person > img:first-child` rule means untagged first-child images get the same styling automatically, so the class is optional when the photo is the first child of the card.

blocks.name: cui-person-name
blocks.summary: Bold name line; size driven by `--cui-person-name-size`.
blocks.description:
Name line inside the person body (`.cui-stack`). Bold, sized at
`--cui-person-name-size` (defaults to `--cui-font-size-1`,
scaled up by the `leadership` variant). Pick a heading element
to fit the surrounding outline (`<h3>` inside a team grid,
`<h2>` on a dedicated bio page).

blocks.name: cui-person-role
blocks.summary: Muted role line beneath the name.
blocks.description:
Role line inside the person body (`.cui-stack`). Muted, sized at
`--cui-person-role-size` (defaults to `--cui-font-size-0`,
scaled up by the `leadership` variant).

blocks.name: cui-person-meta
blocks.summary: Accent-tinted eyebrow label above the name.
blocks.description:
Eyebrow / category label above the name — small-caps, tracked,
accent-tinted. Use for department, team, or status ("Engineering",
"On sabbatical"). Customize the tint via `--cui-person-meta-color`.

blocks.name: cui-person-bio
blocks.summary: Muted bio paragraph beneath the name/role pair.
blocks.description:
Bio paragraph inside the person body (`.cui-stack`). Muted body text with a
small top margin to separate it from the name/role pair. Use
for a two-or-three-sentence summary; longer bios belong on a
dedicated page reached via the card link.

blocks.summary: CSS-only org-chart drawing with SVG-mask connectors.
blocks.description:
Org-chart container — a CSS-only tree drawing that arranges
`.cui-org-node` items vertically by default or horizontally via
`[data-layout="horizontal"]`. Connector lines are drawn by
masking the line color (`--cui-org-line-color`) with embedded
SVG mask images; each connector shape (T-piece, first-child
corner, last-child corner, only-child straight line) is its
own custom property so consumers can swap in curved or dotted
variants without forking the chart logic.

The `dashed` line variant ships a pre-built set of stroke-
dasharray SVGs for the eight connector shapes.

blocks.name: cui-org-node
blocks.summary: One card slot in `.cui-org-chart`; renders its own up-link connector.
blocks.description:
Node inside `.cui-org-chart` — one card slot in the tree. Wraps
a `.cui-person` (or any `.cui-org-card`) child and any nested
`.cui-org-children` list. Connector lines for the up-link to
the parent and any inline-start/end siblings are drawn via
pseudo-elements that pick the right mask image from the chart's
connector set.

blocks.name: cui-org-children
blocks.summary: Nested-children list inside `.cui-org-node`; draws the parent down-link.
blocks.description:
Container for nested `.cui-org-node`s beneath a parent node.
Lays out children inline-start to inline-end (or block-start
to block-end in horizontal layout) with `--cui-org-gap`
between them. Renders the parent's down-link via a `::before`
pseudo-element using the chart's only-child connector SVG.

blocks.summary: Tree node with row, optional children, and attribute-driven state.
blocks.description:
Tree node — a single `<li>` inside a hierarchical `cui-tree`.
Holds a `.cui-tree-row` for the visible row and (when expanded)
a nested `.cui-tree-children` list. Selection, expand/collapse,
and drag-drop state are attribute-driven: `[aria-selected]`,
`[aria-expanded]`, `[data-dragging]`, `[data-drop-position]`. The
`cui-tree` web component manages those attributes; for SSR set
them in markup.

Indentation is `--cui-tree-indent × (--cui-tree-level - 1)`, so
each child node must set its own `--cui-tree-level` (inline
style, SSR-emitted attribute, or via the web component).

blocks.name: cui-tree-toggle
blocks.summary: Expand/collapse caret button inside a tree row.
blocks.description:
Expand/collapse caret inside a `.cui-tree-row`. A small button
with a CSS-drawn chevron that rotates based on the parent
`.cui-tree-item[aria-expanded]` value. Include on leaf nodes too
(without `aria-expanded`) for vertical alignment across rows, or
omit and let the row collapse — both work.

blocks.name: cui-tree-children
blocks.summary: Nested children list; visibility follows `aria-expanded` on the parent.
blocks.description:
Container for nested tree items beneath a `.cui-tree-item`.
`display: none` by default; switches to `display: block` when
the parent `.cui-tree-item` carries `[aria-expanded="true"]`.
Toggling the attribute is enough to expand/collapse — no per-
instance CSS or animation hooks needed.

blocks.summary: Job-opening card (title, tagline, meta, excerpt, actions); composes `.cui-surface` and styles its own descendants.
blocks.description: Vacancy card — a single job opening summarised for a listing or grid. The default layout is a lean single column (eyebrow, title, tagline, meta pills, excerpt, actions) — enough for minimal "title + read more" boards. Add a first-child media element and `[data-orient="row"]` to pair a thumbnail beside the body for richer, image-led recruitment listings. Carries optional `data-team`, `data-location`, `data-contract`, and `data-category` attributes so a filter or `cui-article-filter` can show / hide cards by facet, plus an optional `data-status` (`new`, `closing`, `closed`) that tones the corner pill. Use this directly in HTML or wire it to data from a CMS / ATS; keep the apply link a real `` so listings stay crawlable and work without JS. **Contextual markup.** Compose `.cui-surface` for the card chrome and a `.cui-stack` body; the card styles its own descendants, so each part carries only the canonical primitive (or nothing at all): - first-child `` / `` / `

blocks.summary: Main + aside grid for a vacancy detail page; container-query driven, collapses to one column.
blocks.description:
Two-column layout for a single vacancy detail page: a wide main
column for the narrative body and a narrower aside for the quick
summary, apply CTA, and contact block. Like `.cui-sidebar`, the
split is driven by a **container query** (>= 52rem), so it flips
on the actual container width — pair it with a `.cui-page` /
`.cui-page-wide` ancestor (which sets `container-type:
inline-size`). Below the breakpoint it collapses to one column,
the aside un-sticks, and flows after the body.

**Contextual markup.** The detail styles its own descendants, so the
markup carries only canonical primitives and semantic elements:

- the direct-child `<header>` — the full-width title band (compose
  `.cui-stack`); it carries `.cui-eyebrow` / `.cui-display` /
  `.cui-lead` / `.cui-meta`, retuned to page scale in place.
- a `.cui-flow` / `.cui-prose` child — the narrative body column
  (capped measure); group its copy into plain `<section>`s, each a
  heading plus paragraphs / lists.
- a `.cui-vacancy-aside` child — the summary / apply / contact rail.

blocks.name: cui-vacancy-aside

cssVars.name: --cui-vacancy-aside-top
cssVars.description: Sticky offset from the top. Defaults to `--cui-space-6`.

blocks.summary: Sticky summary / apply / contact rail beside the body; composes `.cui-surface` + `.cui-stack`, styles its own descendants.
blocks.description:
Aside column of `.cui-vacancy-detail`. A bordered rail holding the quick summary, apply CTA, and contact block. Sticks to the top of the viewport on wide layouts so the apply button stays in reach while reading; un-sticks once the layout collapses to a single column. Composes `.cui-surface` for the chrome and `.cui-stack` for the rhythm: `
`. It overrides the surface background (muted, not raised) and drops the shadow for a flatter rail. **Contextual contents.** The aside styles its own descendants: - `.cui-eyebrow` — a section label ("Snel overzicht", "Vragen over deze vacature?"), retinted muted. - a `
` — the quick-facts list: muted `
` labels and bold `
` values, hairline-separated between pairs. - a `.cui-stack` holding a `.cui-button` — the apply block; the button stretches full-width and an optional `

` below is the muted note. - a `.cui-stack` without a button — the contact block; hairline- separated on top, with a bold `` name and `tel:` / `mailto:` links.

blocks.summary: Page container; caps content at 72rem with a 1rem gutter.
blocks.description:
Standard page container. Caps content at 72rem with a 1rem gutter
on either side and `--cui-space-6` padding at the top and bottom.
Establishes a `container-typ: inline-size` context so
child layouts can use container queries (e.g. `cui-sidebar`
flips to two columns at >=48rem container width, not viewport).
Use on the immediate child of `<body>` (or `<main>`) on most pages.

blocks.name: cui-page-narrow
blocks.summary: Page container; caps content at 48rem.
blocks.description:
Narrow page container — caps at 48rem. Use for editorial content
(articles, vacancies, single-column long-form) where line lengths
past 48rem hurt readability. Same gutter and padding as
`.cui-page`, same `container-type: inline-size` for container
queries.

blocks.name: cui-page-wide
blocks.summary: Page container; caps content at 96rem.
blocks.description:
Wide page container — caps at 96rem. Use for dashboards,
data-dense surfaces, or full-bleed marketing pages where the
standard 72rem cap feels cramped. Same gutter and padding as
`.cui-page`, same `container-type: inline-size` for container
queries.

blocks.name: cui-frame
blocks.summary: Full-viewport background frame; surface color + foreground default.
blocks.description:
Full-viewport background frame. Sets `min-block-size: 100vb`
with the default surface color and foreground, so a page using
this class never has the body background showing through during
load. Pair with `.cui-shell` or `.cui-app` inside it for the
actual layout.

blocks.summary: Three-row app shell (header + body + footer).
blocks.description:
Three-row app shell — `header` / `body` / `footer`, sized so
the body takes all available block-axis space. Renders as a
`display: grid` with `grid-template-rows: auto minmax(0, 1fr)
auto`, so the body collapses to fit content but doesn't push
the footer off-screen on short pages.

`[data-fill]` flips to full-block-size mode (used when the
shell is itself inside another scrollable region) — the body
becomes `overflow: auto` instead of letting the page scroll.

blocks.name: cui-shell-body

blocks.description:
Body slot of `.cui-shell`. Sets `min-block-size: 0` so the
body can shrink inside its grid row (preventing forced
overflow). With `[data-fill]` on the shell, the body also
becomes the scroll container.

blocks.name: cui-shell-header
blocks.summary: Sticky raised-surface header for `.cui-shell`.
blocks.description:
Header slot of `.cui-shell`. Sticky to the block-start of the
shell with `z-index: 10`, raised surface background, hairline
border on the block-end. Wrap a `.cui-shell-bar` inside for
the standard inline layout (brand + actions). Shares its
surface chrome with `.cui-shell-footer` via a combined rule.

blocks.name: cui-shell-footer
blocks.summary: Raised-surface footer for `.cui-shell`.
blocks.description:
Footer slot of `.cui-shell`. Raised surface background with a
hairline border on the block-start (mirror of the header).
Wrap a `.cui-shell-bar` for inline layout. Not sticky — just
sits at the block-end of the shell.

blocks.name: cui-shell-bar
blocks.summary: Inline flex bar (brand + actions) inside shell header/footer.
blocks.description:
Inline bar inside `.cui-shell-header` or `.cui-shell-footer`.
Flex row, `min-block-size` from `--cui-shell-bar-size` (4rem
by default), space-between alignment, `--cui-space-4` gap.
Use for the standard brand-on-the-start, actions-on-the-end
pattern.

blocks.summary: Two-row app layout (header + sidebar/main body) with mobile drawer.
blocks.description:
Two-row app layout — `header` / `body`, where the body is a
grid that flips between single-column (mobile) and
sidebar-plus-main (≥48rem) layouts. Designed for dashboards
and admin surfaces with persistent navigation. Above 48rem

to `auto minmax(0, 1fr)` (sidebar + main) at viewports ≥48rem.
The grid template is the only thing that changes between

blocks.name: cui-app-sidebar
blocks.summary: App sidebar; fixed drawer on mobile, inline column at ≥48rem.
blocks.description:
Sidebar slot inside `.cui-app`. On mobile (<48rem), positioned
`fixed` with a slide-in transform and toggles visibility via
`[data-sidebar="open"]` (or `[open]`) on the `.cui-app` root.
At ≥48rem, becomes `position: static` and inline in the
`.cui-app-body` grid. Width comes from `--cui-app-sidebar-size`
(16rem default).

blocks.name: cui-app-sidebar-backdrop
blocks.summary: Scrim behind the mobile sidebar drawer.
blocks.description:
Backdrop scrim behind `.cui-app-sidebar` on mobile. Hidden by
default; shows when `.cui-app` has `[data-sidebar="open"]` (or
`[open]`). At ≥48rem, hidden via `display: none !important`
since the sidebar is no longer a drawer. Click the backdrop in
your JS handler to close the drawer.

blocks.name: cui-app-sidebar-mobile
blocks.summary: Visibility utility for the mobile sidebar trigger button.
blocks.description:
Toggle visibility marker for the mobile sidebar trigger
(typically a hamburger button in the header). `display:
revert` on mobile so it shows; `display: none !important` at
≥48rem so it disappears when the sidebar is inline. Apply to
the trigger button itself.

blocks.name: cui-app-main
blocks.summary: Scrollable main column inside `.cui-app`.
blocks.description:
Main content area inside `.cui-app`. Sets `min-inline-size: 0`
and `min-block-size: 0` so the column can shrink inside its
grid cell (preventing forced overflow), and `overflow: auto`
so long content scrolls within the main area rather than the
page.

blocks.name: cui-page-header
examples:
Components

Interactive primitives, grouped by what they do.
blocks.summary: Page title + lede block (heading sized at `--cui-font-size-3`).
blocks.description:
Page-header section — title + supporting paragraph above the
page body. Heading and paragraph children get
`max-inline-size: 68ch` and `margin: 0`, with headings sized
at `--cui-font-size-3`. Use once near the top of a page,
inside `.cui-page` or `.cui-page-narrow`.

blocks.name: cui-content
blocks.summary: Optimal-measure single-column content (68ch).
blocks.description:
Single-column content measure for long-form text. Caps inline-
size at `--cui-content-size` (68ch default) and applies
vertical rhythm to direct heading / paragraph / list / quote /
pre children. Use inside `.cui-page` when you want the
default 72rem container but a narrower text column.

blocks.name: cui-center
blocks.summary: Centered viewport-sized layout for a single child.
blocks.description:
Centered single-item layout — `display: grid` with `place-items:
center` and `min-block-size: 100vb` so the only child sits at
the visual center of the viewport. Use for sign-in pages, 404
screens, and other single-purpose surfaces.

blocks.name: cui-cover
examples:
Top bar

Centered hero copy

Bottom bar
blocks.summary: Three-row layout: header / centered main / footer.
blocks.description:
Three-row cover layout — `header` / `main` / `footer` where
the main row is vertically centered. Renders as `display:
grid` with `grid-template-rows: auto minmax(0, 1fr) auto`,
sized to `--cui-cover-size` (100vb default). Use for
landing-page heroes that should fill the viewport.

blocks.name: cui-cover-main
blocks.summary: Vertically centered main row inside `.cui-cover`.
blocks.description:
Main row inside `.cui-cover` — vertically centers its content
via `align-content: center`. Apply to the middle child of
`.cui-cover`; header and footer siblings don't need a class.

blocks.name: cui-stack
examples:
Section

First paragraph.

Second paragraph.
blocks.summary: Block-axis stack with `--cui-stack-gap` between children.
blocks.description:
Stack primitive — vertical (block-axis) layout with a
configurable gap between every direct child. Use for any
sequence of block-level content (forms, article paragraphs,
card stacks). Override `--cui-stack-gap` per instance via
inline style or per-scope via a parent rule.

Direct children get `margin-block: 0`, so the gap is the single source of
vertical rhythm — no per-child margin resets needed.

blocks.name: cui-cluster
examples:

  • Blog
blocks.summary: Flex-wrap row with `--cui-cluster-*` controls.
blocks.description:
Cluster primitive — flex-wrap row of items with a configurable
gap and align/justify axes. Use for tags, chip rows, button
groups, breadcrumbs — any inline list where items should wrap
naturally onto multiple rows.

blocks.summary: Auto-fit grid; columns of at least `--cui-grid-min`.
blocks.description:
Auto-fit grid primitive — columns of at least `--cui-grid-min`
(16rem default) that fill the available inline-size. Pin a fixed
column count at ≥48rem with `[data-columns="2|3|4"]`, and tune the
gap with `[data-gap="compact|spacious"]` or `--cui-grid-gap`. Set a
per-content-type column min inline (e.g. 18rem for feature cards,
10rem for stats, 9rem for logos).

blocks.name: cui-switcher

cssVars.name: --cui-switcher-threshold
cssVars.description: Container width at which the layout flips. Defaults to 40rem.

blocks.summary: Row-or-column layout that switches at the configured threshold.
blocks.description:
Switcher primitive — flips between row and column layouts at
the `--cui-switcher-threshold` container width using the
Every-Layout flex-basis trick. Each child gets `flex-basis:
calc((threshold - 100%) * 999)`, which evaluates to a huge
positive when the container is narrower than the threshold
(forcing wrap to column) and a huge negative when wider
(allowing inline layout).

blocks.name: cui-split
examples:
Main content
Side rail
blocks.summary: Single column on mobile → two-column split at ≥48rem.
blocks.description:
Two-column split primitive — single column on mobile, two
columns (with configurable proportions) at ≥48rem. Set
`--cui-split-start` / `--cui-split-end` to control the
column fractions (defaults to 1:1).

blocks.name: cui-sidebar
examples:
\3c !-- table of contents -->
…
blocks.summary: Sidebar + main layout driven by a container query, not a viewport query.
blocks.description:
Sidebar primitive — single column on narrow containers,
sidebar + main on wider containers. Unlike `.cui-split`, this
uses a **container query** (`@container (width >= 48rem)`)
rather than a viewport query, so the layout flips based on the
actual container size, not the viewport. Pair with a `.cui-page`
/ `.cui-page-wide` ancestor (which sets `container-type:
inline-size`) to opt in to container queries.

blocks.name: cui-surface

variants.name: data-radius
variants.description: `none` | `sm` | `md` | `lg`. Presets `--cui-surface-radius` from the radius scale (0 → `--cui-radius-3`); `md` reproduces the default.

variants.name: data-elevation
variants.description: `flat` | `raised` | `floating`. Presets `--cui-surface-shadow` (none → `--cui-shadow-2`); `raised` reproduces the default.

variants.name: data-padding
variants.description: `none` | `sm` | `md` | `lg`. Presets `--cui-surface-padding` from the space scale (0 → `--cui-space-6`); `md` reproduces the default.

blocks.description:
Generic raised surface — the simplest possible card primitive.
Background, border, corner radius, default shadow, default
padding, all driven by override vars so any block can re-skin the
chrome without redeclaring it. Compose it with a layout primitive
(`.cui-stack`, `.cui-cluster`, `.cui-grid`) in markup rather than
reaching for a typed card class.

blocks.name: cui-aside
examples:

Summary copy beside the main column.
blocks.summary: Summary-rail card layout: start-aligned stack that collapses when empty. Compose `.cui-surface` for chrome.
blocks.description:
Aside rail — a summary / sidebar card composed beside the main content: page-intro summary cards, detail-page apply rails, case-study fact panels. A block-axis stack that pins its content to the start of the column and collapses out of flow when it has no children, so an empty rail leaves no gap behind it. Compose `.cui-surface` for the card chrome (set its override vars to retune background / border / padding), and `--cui-aside-gap` for the internal rhythm:
Stack

Combat UI · Vite · TypeScript

blocks.name: cui-meta

variants.name: chips
variants.description: Maps to `[data-variant]`. Renders each item as a muted rounded pill with no separators — for fact rows that read better as discrete tags (job facts: hours, location, salary scale).

cssVars.name: --cui-meta-gap
cssVars.description: Row/column gap between items. Defaults to `--cui-space-2` / `--cui-space-3`.

cssVars.name: --cui-meta-separator
cssVars.description: Glyph between adjacent items. Defaults to `"·"`.

examples:
• 5 min read
  • March 2026
  • Engineering

\3c !-- Chip variant: rounded pills, no separators. -->
  • 32–36 uur
  • Vlaardingen
  • Schaal 5
blocks.summary: Flex-wrap metadata row with `·` separators and list reset.
blocks.description:
Metadata row — one canonical replacement for the per-block `*-meta`
rows (article, event, vacancy, page-intro, person, case). A
flex-wrap row of muted, metadata-sized, strong-weight labels with
list reset baked in, so it works directly on a `<ul>` of `<li>`s or
a row of `<span>`s. Composes the cluster row, a list reset, and the
`.cui-metadata` type into a single class.

Adjacent items are separated by a `·` glyph inserted via `::before`.
Set `--cui-meta-separator` to change the glyph (e.g. `"—"`) or to
`""` to drop separators entirely. Emphasize a single entry (byline,
time) by giving it full-strength color in markup, not extra weight.

blocks.name: cui-visually-hidden
blocks.summary: Screen-reader-only utility (visually hidden, still announced).
blocks.description:
Visually hides an element while keeping it announced by screen
readers. Use for skip-link labels, off-screen instructions, and
status text ("Showing X of Y") that you don't want on screen but
do want in the accessibility tree.

blocks.name: cui-text-muted
blocks.summary: Sets foreground to `--cui-color-muted`.
blocks.description:
Applies the secondary text color (`--cui-color-muted`). Use on
inline supporting text — short captions, inline metadata, helper
sentences — when no semantic class already covers it. Prefer the
semantic option (`.cui-caption`, `.cui-metadata`,
`.cui-alert-message`) where one exists.

blocks.name: cui-text-balance
blocks.summary: Balances line breaks across wrapped text (headings, quotes).
blocks.description:
Enables `text-wrap: balance` so the renderer evens line lengths
across wrapped text. Best for short headings and pull quotes;
the browser implementation caps out around six lines. Don't apply
to body paragraphs — the layout cost outweighs the visual win.

blocks.name: cui-full-bleed
examples:
Lead paragraph inside the prose measure.

Back inside the prose measure.
blocks.summary: Breaks out of the parent container to span the viewport.
blocks.description:
Escapes a constrained ancestor and spans the full viewport's
inline-size. Use sparingly on media inside `.cui-container` or
`.cui-prose` — full-bleed images, hero bands, accent strips. The
negative-margin trick assumes the element is a direct child of
the container; don't nest deeper.

blocks.name: cui-size-sm
blocks.summary: Scales interactive controls to ~88% via `--cui-control-scale`.
blocks.description:
Scales nested interactive controls (`.cui-button`, form controls,
etc.) down to ~88% of their default size by setting
`--cui-control-scale: 0.88`. Apply to a container to scope the
change, or directly on a single control.

blocks.name: cui-size-lg
blocks.summary: Scales interactive controls to ~116% via `--cui-control-scale`.
blocks.description:
Scales nested interactive controls up to ~116% of their default
size by setting `--cui-control-scale: 1.16`. Pair with
`.cui-density-comfortable` for an intentionally oversized cluster
(landing-page CTAs, onboarding screens).

blocks.name: cui-density-compact
blocks.summary: Sets `--cui-density: 0` for tight control sizing.
blocks.description:
Sets `--cui-density: 0`, collapsing the density component of
`--cui-control-size`. Use on data-heavy surfaces (dashboards,
admin tables) where padding wastes screen real estate. Combine
with `.cui-size-sm` for the tightest possible UI.

blocks.name: cui-density-comfortable
blocks.summary: Sets `--cui-density: 1.25` for touch-friendly control sizing.
blocks.description:
Sets `--cui-density: 1.25`, adding a touch-friendly margin to
`--cui-control-size`. Use on touch-first surfaces (kiosks,
mobile-only flows) or marketing pages where controls should read
as inviting rather than utilitarian.
