blocks.summary: Page-level surface; min-block-size + default colors.
blocks.description:
Page-level surface — apply to `<body>` or the outermost wrapper
to set the default page background and foreground color from
the surface tokens. Pairs with the theme overrides in
tokens.css so the page background follows the active theme
without any per-page wiring.

blocks.name: cui-container

cssVars.name: --cui-container-size
cssVars.description: Override the container max-width inline (or via a parent class). Defaults to `--cui-container`.

examples:
Section title

Content inside the section, capped at 72rem.
blocks.summary: Centered horizontal container; max-width + fluid gutter.
blocks.description:
Horizontal container primitive. Centers content with
`margin-inline: auto`, caps inline-size at `--cui-container-size`
(which itself defaults to `--cui-container`, 72rem), and applies
a fluid gutter on each side via `--cui-container-gutter`.
Establishes a `container-type: inline-size` context so child
layouts (`.cui-sidebar`, content-grid family) can use container
queries.

Use this as the inline-axis wrapper inside a `.cui-section`,
`.cui-hero`, or any full-bleed surface where you want capped
content. Combine with `.cui-container-narrow` / `.cui-container-wide`
for the alternate widths.

blocks.name: cui-container-narrow
examples: …
blocks.summary: Narrow container modifier; sets `--cui-container-size` to 48rem.
blocks.description:
Narrow container variant — overrides `--cui-container-size` to
`--cui-container-narrow` (48rem). Use as a modifier alongside
`.cui-container` for editorial content that wants a tighter
measure (articles, vacancies, single-column long-form).

blocks.name: cui-container-wide
blocks.summary: Wide container modifier; sets `--cui-container-size` to 96rem.
blocks.description:
Wide container variant — overrides `--cui-container-size` to
`--cui-container-wide` (96rem). Use as a modifier alongside
`.cui-container` for dashboards, full-bleed marketing surfaces,
or any layout where the standard 72rem cap feels cramped.

blocks.name: cui-section

variants.name: compact
variants.description: Maps to `[data-spacing]`. Switches `padding-block` to the matching `--cui-section-space-*` clamp. Aliased by `.cui-section-compact`, `.cui-section-spacious`, and `.cui-section-hero` for class-based composition.

variants.name: spacious
variants.description: Maps to `[data-spacing]`. Switches `padding-block` to the matching `--cui-section-space-*` clamp. Aliased by `.cui-section-compact`, `.cui-section-spacious`, and `.cui-section-hero` for class-based composition.

variants.name: hero
variants.description: Maps to `[data-spacing]`. Switches `padding-block` to the matching `--cui-section-space-*` clamp. Aliased by `.cui-section-compact`, `.cui-section-spacious`, and `.cui-section-hero` for class-based composition.

examples: Default section

\3c !-- Hero spacing, accent tone, capped content -->

Landing-page hero

A balanced lead paragraph.
blocks.summary: Page-level section; fluid block padding via `--cui-section-space`.
blocks.description:
Page-level section primitive — applies `--cui-section-space`
(a fluid clamp that scales 4–8rem) as block-axis padding so
sections breathe consistently across viewport sizes. Use on
`<section>` elements that divide the page; pair with
`.cui-container` inside to cap the content's inline-size.

Spacing rhythm comes in three additional sizes via
`[data-spacing]` (or the matching `.cui-section-compact` /
`-spacious` / `-hero` modifier classes — both forms work).
Tone comes via the separate `.cui-section-muted` /
`.cui-section-inverse` / `.cui-section-accent` modifier
classes; combine spacing and tone freely.

blocks.name: cui-section-compact
blocks.summary: Compact section spacing (`--cui-section-space-compact`).
blocks.description:
Compact spacing modifier for `.cui-section`. Sets
`padding-block` to `--cui-section-space-compact` (clamp 2.5–5rem).
Use on tightly-packed sections like FAQ lists, logo strips,
and short interstitial bands.

Equivalent to `[data-spacing="compact"]` on `.cui-section` —
both forms produce identical output.

blocks.name: cui-section-spacious
blocks.summary: Spacious section spacing (`--cui-section-space-spacious`).
blocks.description:
Spacious spacing modifier for `.cui-section`. Sets
`padding-block` to `--cui-section-space-spacious` (clamp
5.5–11rem). Use on marketing sections and editorial features
that need extra breathing room.

Equivalent to `[data-spacing="spacious"]` on `.cui-section`.

blocks.name: cui-section-hero
blocks.summary: Hero-sized section spacing (`--cui-section-space-hero`).
blocks.description:
Hero spacing modifier for `.cui-section`. Sets `padding-block`
to `--cui-section-space-hero` (clamp 6–14rem). Use on
landing-page heroes and other top-of-page openings that should
dominate the fold.

Equivalent to `[data-spacing="hero"]` on `.cui-section`.

blocks.name: cui-section-muted
blocks.summary: Muted background tone for a `.cui-section`.
blocks.description:
Muted-tone modifier for `.cui-section`. Applies
`--cui-color-surface-muted` as the section background — useful
for visually separating sections without changing typography
or contrast.

blocks.name: cui-section-inverse
blocks.summary: Inverse (dark) background tone for a `.cui-section`.
blocks.description:
Inverse-tone modifier for `.cui-section`. Flips the section to
a dark surface with light foreground — same colors the dark
theme uses for the page itself, but applied to a single
section. Pair with `.cui-eyebrow` / `.cui-display` / `.cui-lead`
inside; their `--cui-color-inverse-*` overrides take over for
the right contrast.

blocks.name: cui-section-accent
blocks.summary: Accent-colored background for a `.cui-section`.
blocks.description:
Accent-tone modifier for `.cui-section`. Paints the section
with `--cui-color-accent` and switches foreground to
`--cui-color-on-accent`. Use sparingly — accent sections
compete with primary CTAs for visual weight, so reserve for
deliberate brand moments (top-of-page promos, conversion
bands).

blocks.name: cui-flow

cssVars.name: --cui-flow-space
cssVars.description: Gap between direct children (read on the *next* child). Defaults to `--cui-space-4`.

examples:
Article title

First paragraph.

Second paragraph.

Subsection

Subsection paragraph (larger gap before this h3).
blocks.summary: Owl-selector vertical rhythm utility.
blocks.description:
Vertical-rhythm utility — applies a consistent
`--cui-flow-space` gap (default `--cui-space-4`) between
every direct child via the "owl selector" (`> * + *`). First
child gets no top margin; every subsequent sibling gets the
flow space as `margin-block-start`. Same pattern as Heydon
Pickering's Every-Layout flow.

Use on content where children have varied semantics (headings,
paragraphs, lists, figures) and you want a uniform rhythm
without enumerating every child selector. Override per-element
via inline `style="--cui-flow-space: …"` on the next sibling
to insert a bigger gap (the value reads on the *next* child).

blocks.name: cui-prose
examples:
Article title

Lead paragraph that sets the tone.

Section heading

Body paragraph with the standard measure.

  • List item.
  • Another list item.
blocks.summary: Long-form prose container with editorial typography hierarchy.
blocks.description:
Long-form prose container. Caps inline-size at
`--cui-prose-measure` (72ch default) and applies the editorial
typography hierarchy to direct heading / paragraph / list /
quote / pre children:

  - Headings get tight line-height and `text-wrap: balance`;
    `<h1>` is sized at `--cui-font-size-5`, `<h2>` at `-4`.
  - Paragraphs and list items get the muted color.
  - Every direct child gets `margin-block: 0 --cui-space-4`
    so the bottom margin handles vertical rhythm.

Use for blog posts, case studies, vacancy detail pages — any
long-form text content. Wrap inside `.cui-container-narrow`
for the canonical editorial measure.

blocks.name: cui-display
blocks.summary: Display-scale headline class; capped at 13ch.
blocks.description:
Display headline class — `--cui-font-size-display` (5.5rem at
peak), display weight (760), tight line-height (0.98),
`text-wrap: balance`, and a 13ch max-inline-size so the
headline wraps to two or three lines on typical viewports.
Use on landing-page hero headlines and other display-scale
moments.

Each axis takes an override var so a block can compose the headline
and retune it (e.g. a card title at a smaller scale) by setting the
var rather than redeclaring the rule — the same pattern as
`--cui-surface-*`. Defaults reproduce the original display look.

blocks.name: cui-lead
blocks.summary: Lead paragraph; sized at `--cui-font-size-2`, muted.
blocks.description:
Lead paragraph class — `--cui-font-size-2` (1.5rem at peak),
muted color, looser line-height (1.4), capped at the content
measure (68ch). Use directly under a hero or article headline
for the lead paragraph; visually distinguishes the opening
from the body that follows.

Size, line-height, and measure each take an override var so a block
can compose the lead and retune it (e.g. a smaller card subtitle) by
setting the var rather than redeclaring the rule. Defaults reproduce
the original lead look.

blocks.name: cui-eyebrow
examples:
For agencies

Ship sites that survive accessibility audits

A lead sentence below.
blocks.summary: Small-caps accent-tinted label above a title (`.cui-kicker` is an alias).
blocks.description:
Eyebrow / kicker class — small-caps, accent-tinted, tracked
(`letter-spacing: 0.08em`), uppercase. Both `.cui-eyebrow` and
`.cui-kicker` are aliases for the same styling — pick the
name that fits the editorial intent (eyebrow above a title,
kicker as a pre-title hook).

blocks.name: cui-caption
blocks.summary: Muted caption text; sized at `--cui-font-size-caption`.
blocks.description:
Caption text class — muted, caption-sized
(`--cui-font-size-caption`). Use on `<figcaption>`, image
captions, and small descriptive text below media. For inline
metadata (date, byline, reading time), use `.cui-metadata`
instead — bolder weight, same size.

blocks.name: cui-metadata
blocks.summary: Muted bold metadata text.
blocks.description:
Metadata text class — muted, metadata-sized
(`--cui-font-size-metadata`), bold. Use for inline metadata
lines (author + date + reading time, location + role, etc.)
where caption-weight is too soft.

blocks.name: cui-skip-link
examples:
Skip to content
…
…
blocks.summary: Keyboard-accessible "skip to content" link.
blocks.description:
Skip-to-content link — visually hidden by default (translated
out of the viewport), revealed when focused via keyboard so
users can jump straight to `<main>` without tabbing through
the navigation. Place as the **first** focusable element
inside `<body>`, with `href="#main"` (or whichever ID your
main landmark uses).

Accent background, on-accent foreground; matches the focus
ring's accent tint so keyboard users see the link reveal as a
deliberate, branded affordance.

tokenGroups.name: typography

tokens.name: --cui-font-family
tokens.description: Default sans stack. Inter first, system fallbacks.

tokenGroups.summary: Font family, fluid size scale, line heights, weights.
tokenGroups.description:
Typography scale and weights. Sizes use `clamp()` so they scale fluidly
between roughly 320px and 1280px viewports; pick the closest size by
editorial role, not pixel target. Weights pair with the variable Inter
font — `regular` for body, `strong` for emphasis and most headings,
`display` for hero titles only.

tokenGroups.name: color-base

tokens.name: --cui-color-on-accent
tokens.description: Foreground when sitting on an accent fill.

tokens.name: --cui-color-raised
tokens.description: Pure white. Use sparingly when `surface-raised` isn't enough.

tokenGroups.summary: Light-theme color tokens (foreground, surfaces, semantic accents).
tokenGroups.description:
Color tokens for the default light theme, plus the `color-scheme`
declaration that tells the browser the page supports both light and
dark (so native form controls and scrollbars adapt). The surface
tokens form a three-step ladder (surface → surface-muted →
surface-raised) for back-to-front depth. The `inverse-*` tokens are
declared only in the theme overrides — don't reach for them here.

tokenGroups.name: spacing
tokenGroups.description:
Spacing scale, in rem. Steps are roughly geometric — each is 1.5–2×
the previous one. Use these in every spacing property; bespoke pixel
values should be rare. Compose larger spaces by jumping a level
rather than stacking smaller values.

tokenGroups.name: section-spacing
tokenGroups.summary: Page-level vertical rhythm clamps (compact / default / spacious / hero).
tokenGroups.description:
Vertical rhythm tokens for page-level section padding. Each value is
a `clamp()` that scales between a mobile minimum and a large-desktop
maximum. Pick by editorial intent, not by screen size — `compact` for
tightly packed FAQ/logo blocks, `hero` for landing-page openings.

tokenGroups.name: surface
tokenGroups.summary: Radii, border thickness, and shadow scale for component surfaces.
tokenGroups.description:
Surface-treatment tokens used across every component: corner radii,
border thickness, and the two-step shadow scale. Radii are kept small
to match Combat UI's flat aesthetic; shadows are tuned to read well
against both light and dark surfaces.

tokenGroups.name: control

tokens.name: --cui-control-size
tokens.description: Default interactive control min-block-size. Computed from `--cui-density` and `--cui-control-scale`; resolves to 2.5rem at density 1.

tokens.name: --cui-control-radius
tokens.description: Corner radius for controls. Defaults to `--cui-radius-1`.

tokenGroups.description:
Interactive control sizing and focus-ring tokens. `--cui-control-size`
is computed from the `--cui-density` and `--cui-control-scale`
`@property` inputs declared at the top of this file — set those to
scale the entire control surface up or down without redefining
individual heights. Focus-ring tokens are consumed by every focusable
control via the shared `outline` declarations.

tokenGroups.name: containers

tokens.name: --cui-container-gutter
tokens.description: clamp(1rem, 4vi, 2rem). Side gutter outside the container.

tokenGroups.summary: Page container max widths and gutter.
tokenGroups.description:
Maximum content widths for page-level containers. `.cui-container`
uses `--cui-container` as its max-inline-size with a fluid gutter on
each side. The narrow/wide variants are for tighter editorial content
(articles, case studies) and dashboard / full-bleed layouts.

tokenGroups.name: content-measures
tokenGroups.description:
Optimal-reading-width tokens. `prose-measure` (72ch) is the broad
comfortable line; `content-measure` / `content-size` (68ch) is the
standard `.cui-content` cap. The center/cover sizes drive full-
viewport layouts (intro screens, takeovers).

tokenGroups.name: layout

tokens.name: --cui-switcher-threshold
tokens.description: Container width at which `.cui-switcher` flips from row to column. Defaults to 40rem.

tokenGroups.summary: Tokens for stack / cluster / grid / switcher / split / flow / content-grid primitives.
tokenGroups.description:
Tokens for the Every-Layout-style primitives in `layout.css` —
`.cui-stack`, `.cui-cluster`, `.cui-grid`, `.cui-switcher`,
`.cui-split`, `.cui-flow`, and `.cui-content-grid`. Each primitive
consumes a small set to control gaps, thresholds, and proportions.
Override per-instance via inline `style="--cui-stack-gap: …"` or
per-scope via a parent rule — custom properties inherit, so a
section-level override flows into every nested primitive.

tokenGroups.name: shell
tokenGroups.description:
Sizing tokens for the app-shell primitives in `layout.css`. Use
`.cui-shell` and `.cui-app` to compose top-bar / sidebar / main
regions; these tokens set the bar height and sidebar width.

tokenGroups.name: color-dark

tokens.name: --cui-color-on-accent
tokens.description: Foreground when on an accent fill.

tokens.name: --cui-color-inverse
tokens.description: Foreground when reading against an inverse surface.

tokens.name: --cui-color-inverse-muted
tokens.description: Secondary text on an inverse surface.

tokens.name: --cui-color-inverse-surface
tokens.description: Inverse surface (light block on a dark page).

tokens.name: --cui-color-inverse-eyebrow
tokens.description: Eyebrow / kicker color on inverse surfaces.

tokenGroups.summary: Dark-theme overrides for color and surface tokens.
tokenGroups.description:
Dark-theme color overrides applied when the user explicitly picks
dark mode via `[data-theme="dark"]` on `<html>` / `<body>`. The same
set of overrides also applies automatically via the
`@media (prefers-color-scheme: dark)` block above when no explicit
theme is pinned — that block isn't separately documented because its
content is identical to this one.

Sets `color-scheme: dark` so native form controls and scrollbars
adapt. The `--cui-color-inverse-*` group exists only in the theme
overrides — use it for elements that visually invert relative to the
page (a dark CTA strip on a light page, a light callout on a dark
page, etc.).

tokenGroups.name: color-light

tokens.name: --cui-color-on-accent
tokens.description: Foreground when on an accent fill.

tokenGroups.summary: Light-theme overrides for color and surface tokens (explicit opt-in).
tokenGroups.description:
Light-theme color overrides applied when the user explicitly picks
light mode via `[data-theme="light"]` — useful for forcing light on
a specific surface (a docs page inside a dark dashboard, an opt-out
for users who prefer dark globally but not here).

Mirrors `color-dark` with light-side values. The `--cui-color-inverse-*`
tokens here are darker tones, since on a light page the "inverse"
surface is dark.
